# 🎉 Production Waves Restructuring - Complete\n\n**All done! Republic Studio has been restructured around Production Waves.**\n\nThis document summarizes what was created and updated.\n\n---\n\n## What Changed\n\n### Before\n```\nPhases (generic, passive naming)\n├─ Phase 1: Foundation\n├─ Phase 2: World Building\n├─ Phase 3: Advanced Systems\n├─ Phase 4: Intelligence\n├─ Phase 5: Multiplayer\n└─ Phase 6: Presentation\n```\n\n### After\n```\nProduction Waves (active, strategic naming)\n├─ WAVE 0: FOUNDATION → \"The skeleton works\"\n├─ WAVE 1: SIMULATION CORE → \"The world lives without a player\"\n├─ WAVE 2: DECISION INTERFACE → \"The player has an office\"\n├─ WAVE 3: EMERGENCE & DEPTH → \"Every choice matters and echoes\"\n├─ WAVE 4: INTELLIGENCE & AUTONOMY → \"The world doesn't need you to survive\"\n├─ WAVE 5: MULTIPLAYER & EMERGENCE → \"Politics becomes personal\"\n└─ WAVE 6: PRESENTATION & POLISH → \"The world looks like leadership\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## Documents Created\n\n### 🏛️ Core Strategy Documents (.github/)\n\n#### 1. [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n**What**: Complete Production Waves strategy document\n**Purpose**: Defines all 7 waves, objectives, systems, milestones, and philosophy\n**Size**: ~8,200 words\n**Audience**: Everyone\n**Use**: Reference guide for development strategy\n\n#### 2. [REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md)\n**What**: Complete Republic Studio overview\n**Purpose**: Explains how GitHub is set up as Republic Studio, complete wave progression\n**Size**: ~13,100 words\n**Audience**: Everyone (especially new team members)\n**Use**: Understanding the big picture\n\n#### 3. [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)\n**What**: Step-by-step GitHub setup guide\n**Purpose**: How to configure GitHub for Republic Studio (boards, milestones, labels, etc)\n**Size**: ~13,500 words\n**Audience**: Project managers, team leads\n**Use**: Actually implementing Republic Studio in GitHub\n\n#### 4. [.github/README.md](./.github/README.md)\n**What**: Navigation and overview of .github/ documents\n**Purpose**: Quick reference, role-based navigation\n**Size**: ~11,000 words\n**Audience**: Everyone\n**Use**: Knowing which document to read for your role\n\n---\n\n### 📚 Updated Documentation (Docs/)\n\n#### 5. [Docs/README.md](../Docs/README.md)\n**What**: Updated main documentation hub\n**Changes**:\n- Added Production Waves overview\n- Updated feature descriptions\n- Added wave-based workflow section\n- Emphasized GitHub integration\n- Linked to PRODUCTION_WAVES.md\n\n#### 6. [Docs/Roadmap/README.md](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md)\n**What**: Updated Roadmap\n**Changes**:\n- Replaced \"Phases\" with \"Waves\"\n- Updated all descriptions to wave language\n- Added wave completion gates\n- Added timeline by wave\n- Updated wave descriptions with specific systems\n- Added release strategy section\n\n#### 7. [Docs/Features/README.md](../Docs/Features/README.md)\n**What**: Updated Features documentation\n**Changes**:\n- Organized by Production Waves instead of Phases\n- Added Wave 0-6 structure\n- Listed systems per Wave\n- Added milestone gates\n- Linked to PRODUCTION_WAVES.md\n- Added Republic Test reminder\n\n---\n\n## Total Documentation Created\n\n| Document | Type | Purpose | Size |\n|----------|------|---------|------|\n| PRODUCTION_WAVES.md | Strategy | Development approach | 8.2 KB |\n| REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md | Overview | How everything works | 13.1 KB |\n| REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md | Setup Guide | Implement Republic Studio | 13.5 KB |\n| .github/README.md | Navigation | Quick reference | 11 KB |\n| Docs/README.md | Updated | Hub with Wave links | 7 KB |\n| Docs/Roadmap/README.md | Updated | Timeline by Wave | 9.6 KB |\n| Docs/Features/README.md | Updated | Features by Wave | 6.2 KB |\n| **TOTAL** | **7 documents** | **Complete strategy** | **~68.6 KB** |\n\n---\n\n## Key Changes Summary\n\n### Terminology\n✅ Changed \"Phases\" → \"Production Waves\"\n✅ Added descriptive Wave names (Foundation, Simulation Core, etc)\n✅ Added milestone gates (\"The skeleton works\", \"The world lives without a player\", etc)\n✅ Changed from passive to active language (\"Build nothing visible\" vs \"Complete Phase 1\")\n\n### Structure\n✅ One clear Wave progression (0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6)\n✅ Each Wave has clear objective\n✅ Each Wave has specific systems to build\n✅ Each Wave has measurable completion gate\n✅ Each Wave enables the next\n\n### Documentation\n✅ Created comprehensive Wave strategy document\n✅ Created GitHub setup guide\n✅ Updated Roadmap with Wave structure\n✅ Updated Features with Wave organization\n✅ Updated Docs hub to emphasize Waves\n✅ Added role-based navigation guides\n\n### Philosophy\n✅ GitHub is now the primary tool (not just code repo)\n✅ Issues labeled by Wave\n✅ Project boards created per Wave\n✅ Milestones represent Wave completion gates\n✅ Documentation linked to every issue\n✅ One source of truth (GitHub)\n\n---\n\n## How to Use This\n\n### Immediately\n1. ✅ Read [REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md)\n2. ✅ Share with team\n3. ✅ Follow [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md) to implement\n\n### For Developers\n1. Read [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md) for strategy\n2. Check [Docs/Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md) for current Wave\n3. Open current Wave project board\n4. Pick an issue\n5. Build!\n\n### For Project Managers\n1. Follow [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)\n2. Create Wave boards in GitHub\n3. Create Wave milestones\n4. Create Wave labels\n5. Link existing issues\n6. Track progress via boards and milestones\n\n### For Designers\n1. Read [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md) for strategy\n2. Review [Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)\n3. Contribute to [Features](../Docs/Features/README.md) per Wave\n4. Link design docs to issues\n\n### For New Team Members\n1. Read [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md) (2 min)\n2. Read [REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md) (10 min)\n3. Read [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md) (10 min)\n4. Check [Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md) for current Wave\n5. Start building!\n\n---\n\n## The Wave Structure\n\n```\nWAVE 0: FOUNDATION (2-3 weeks)\n  Build nothing visible. Only architecture.\n  Milestone: \"The skeleton works\"\n  Systems: Bootstrap, Logging, Event Bus, Time Engine, etc\n\nWAVE 1: SIMULATION CORE (3-4 weeks)\n  Build the world without presentation.\n  Milestone: \"The world lives without a player\"\n  Systems: World Manager, Countries, Geography, Economy, etc\n\nWAVE 2: DECISION INTERFACE (4-6 weeks)\n  Build how players make decisions.\n  Milestone: \"The player has an office\"\n  Systems: Workspace, Advisors, Cabinet, Reports, Communications, etc\n\nWAVE 3: EMERGENCE & DEPTH (6-8 weeks)\n  Build interconnected consequences.\n  Milestone: \"Every choice matters and echoes\"\n  Systems: Legislature, Factions, Diplomacy, Military, Trade, etc\n\nWAVE 4: INTELLIGENCE & AUTONOMY (4-6 weeks)\n  Build thinking players (AI).\n  Milestone: \"The world doesn't need you to survive\"\n  Systems: AI Decision Engine, Economic AI, Political AI, etc\n\nWAVE 5: MULTIPLAYER & EMERGENCE (4-6 weeks)\n  Build multiplayer politics.\n  Milestone: \"Politics becomes personal\"\n  Systems: Networking, Server, Synchronization, Diplomacy, etc\n\nWAVE 6: PRESENTATION & POLISH (4-6 weeks)\n  Build what you see.\n  Milestone: \"The world looks like leadership\"\n  Systems: 3D Office, Animation, Audio, Cinematics, Polish, etc\n\nTOTAL: ~39 weeks to release\n```\n\n---\n\n## GitHub Setup (Action Items)\n\nTo fully implement Republic Studio, follow [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md):\n\n### Boards\n- [ ] Create Wave 0 - Foundation board\n- [ ] Create Wave 1 - Simulation Core board\n- [ ] Create Wave 2 - Decision Interface board\n- [ ] Create Wave 3 - Emergence & Depth board\n- [ ] Create Wave 4 - Intelligence & Autonomy board\n- [ ] Create Wave 5 - Multiplayer & Emergence board\n- [ ] Create Wave 6 - Presentation & Polish board\n- [ ] Create Active Sprint (master) board\n\n### Milestones\n- [ ] Create Wave 0 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 1 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 2 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 3 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 4 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 5 Complete milestone\n- [ ] Create Wave 6 Complete milestone\n\n### Labels\n- [ ] Create Wave-0-Foundation label\n- [ ] Create Wave-1-Simulation label\n- [ ] Create Wave-2-Interface label\n- [ ] Create Wave-3-Emergence label\n- [ ] Create Wave-4-Intelligence label\n- [ ] Create Wave-5-Multiplayer label\n- [ ] Create Wave-6-Presentation label\n- [ ] Create Priority labels (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n- [ ] Create Type labels (epic, feature, bug, chore, docs)\n\n### Issues\n- [ ] Tag all issues with Wave label\n- [ ] Assign issues to Wave project boards\n- [ ] Assign issues to Wave milestones\n- [ ] Link issues to design documentation\n\n---\n\n## Success Indicators\n\n**Republic Studio is working when:**\n\n✅ All Wave documentation created and shared\n✅ GitHub boards set up per Wave\n✅ Milestones representing Wave gates created\n✅ Issues labeled by Wave\n✅ Team understands wave progression\n✅ Daily standup references Wave boards\n✅ Developers moving issues across columns\n✅ Velocity tracked per Wave\n✅ Wave 0 progressing on schedule\n✅ Next Wave planned before current Wave ends\n✅ Release timeline clear\n✅ Momentum building through Waves\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps\n\n### This Week\n1. ✅ Read all documentation (you're doing it!)\n2. ✅ Share with team\n3. ✅ Discuss Production Waves strategy\n4. ✅ Align on terminology (\"Waves\" not \"Phases\")\n\n### Next Week\n1. ✅ Follow [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)\n2. ✅ Create Wave boards in GitHub\n3. ✅ Create milestones\n4. ✅ Create labels\n5. ✅ Tag existing issues\n\n### Ongoing\n1. ✅ Use Wave boards daily\n2. ✅ Move issues across columns\n3. ✅ Track velocity\n4. ✅ Achieve Wave completion gates\n5. ✅ Celebrate Wave milestones\n6. ✅ Progress through all 7 Waves\n7. ✅ Ship Republic! 🚀\n\n---\n\n## Reference\n\n### Strategic Documents\n- [UX Documentation](../Docs/UX/README.md) - Player experience\n\n---\n\n## The Quote\n\n> \"Republic is not a game about ruling people. It is a game about carrying the burden of democratic leadership.\"\n\nEvery Production Wave serves this truth.\n\n---\n\n## Timeline\n\n```\nWeek 1-3:   WAVE 0 - Foundation             🟡 Now\n            \"The skeleton works\"\n               ↓\nWeek 4-7:   WAVE 1 - Simulation Core       🔴 Next\n            \"The world lives without a player\"\n               ↓\nWeek 8-13:  WAVE 2 - Decision Interface    🔴 Planned\n            \"The player has an office\"\n               ↓\nWeek 14-21: WAVE 3 - Emergence & Depth     🔴 Planned\n            \"Every choice matters and echoes\"\n               ↓\nWeek 22-27: WAVE 4 - Intelligence & AI     🔴 Planned\n            \"The world doesn't need you to survive\"\n               ↓\nWeek 28-33: WAVE 5 - Multiplayer           🔴 Planned\n            \"Politics becomes personal\"\n               ↓\nWeek 34-39: WAVE 6 - Presentation          🔴 Planned\n            \"The world looks like leadership\"\n               ↓\n            🎉 RELEASE READY\n```\n\n---\n\n## Commit History\n\n✅ Added [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n✅ Updated [Docs/Roadmap/README.md](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md)\n✅ Updated [Docs/Features/README.md](../Docs/Features/README.md)\n✅ Updated [Docs/README.md](../Docs/README.md)\n✅ Added [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)\n✅ Added [REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md)\n✅ Added [.github/README.md](./.github/README.md)\n✅ Added this completion summary\n\n---\n\n## Final Note\n\n**Republic is now structured around Production Waves.**\n\nGitHub is the studio. Issues are tasks. Boards are sprints. Milestones are gates. Discussions are strategy. Documentation is linked.\n\nEverything is connected. One source of truth.\n\n**Now let's build Republic. Wave by Wave. Gate by gate.**\n\n**Start at Wave 0. Take it home.**\n\n🚀\n"
